danger_signals.visual_form: Non-humanoid extraterrestrial sprite designs distinct from era-appropriate human figures
danger_signals.meaning: Otherworldly threat; invasion force; hostile faction

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, more elaborately detailed alien sprites appearing at era endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger threshold; mandatory confrontation; level-ending threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Real-time clock pressure (36 minutes 30 seconds for full playthrough; one game day = six seconds)
danger_signals.meaning: Existential danger; self-destruct timer on player character; failure state approaching

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles contextual to each era (Medieval traps, Western frontier dangers, naval threats, WW2 ordnance)
danger_signals.meaning: Era-specific dangers layered with alien invasion threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible wrapped objects scattered throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition; player empowerment; progression enablement

safety_signals.visual_form: Five glowing spheres per era level; one dropped by Drakkon Lord boss
safety_signals.meaning: Progression gate; time machine power; mandatory objectives; safe milestone completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Present-day staging area; return point between era missions
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; narrative anchor; operational base
Hazard mitigation; failure recovery; resource buffer

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard Drakkon soldiers appear as baseline alien sprites; Drakkon Lords as enlarged, more ornate variants; Drakkon King as final boss form in year 3000
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien invasion forces manifest in each historical era despite anachronistic presence; contextual adaptation to each period while maintaining alien visual identity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Player character (Time Lord); humanoid protagonist sprite
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: MB Time Travel Research Centre as human operational nexus; era-period civilians/NPCs as implicit human populations under threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Era-appropriate architectural, technological, and environmental aesthetics (Medieval castles, Caribbean ships, Western frontier towns, WW2 France); alien forces visually incongruent with setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Drakkon forces occupy and patrol historical locations; civilians/period NPCs implicit as threatened populations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien enemies present throughout each period; mixed visual language of historical setting contaminated by extraterrestrial presence; oblique isometric perspective emphasizes 3D spatial occupation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Drakkon units patrol, attack, defend orbs; boss entities stationary or territorially aggressive; time pressure intensifies visual urgency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss defeated; orb collected; visual transition toward next historical period or final confrontation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player progression allowed; time machine powered incrementally; path to present day opened

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return to MB Time Travel Research Centre; Drakkon King encounter; high-stakes final confrontation visual
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss battle; existential threat (self-destruct timer reaching completion); victory/failure determination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual/audio pressure indicators (likely tonal shift, sprite flickering, or HUD urgency cues)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must complete remaining objectives before self-destruct; no-recovery failure state imminent

environmental_storytelling.example: Medieval England (1250) level visually establishes stone castles, period weapons (swords); Caribbean (1650) features ships/naval setting; Western US (1860) shows frontier towns/guns; France (1943) depicts WW2 military aesthetic. Alien invasion visually intrudes upon these historically-grounded spaces.
Player acquires period-appropriate weapons (swords → firearms → grenades → lasers) as both mechanical progression and narrative signal of technological evolution from medieval to futuristic warfare.
Eliminating Drakkon Lords in each era visually represents restoration of historical timeline; orb collection signals successful thwarting of alien temporal sabotage.

Real-time clock pressure (36:30 gameplay window) converts abstract time-travel plot into constant visual/mechanical threat; creates urgency narrative without explicit HUD countdown necessarily visible.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves through level terrain; guards collectibles and pathways
Attacks player on sight; projectile or melee engagement
Occupies boss arena; defends golden orb; elevated combat threat; multi-hit defeat condition
Year 3000 confrontation; ultimate invasion force leader; defeat triggers game victory
Offensive capability; weapon-dependent damage output; can progress through enemy territory
Restricted to melee punch; must locate weapon packages; vulnerable state incentivizes rapid armament
Must accelerate mission completion; cannot delay in any era; self-destruct consequence looms

notes: Time Lord uses temporal invasion as core worldbuilding device. Visual communication relies on era authenticity contrast with alien incongruity—historical periods serve as recognizable contexts that alien presence violates, making invasion visually legible without explicit dialogue. Oblique isometric graphics support 3D spatial invasion narrative (alien forces occupying real-world spaces). Deadline mechanic (36:30 window) is the primary non-narrative danger signal, converting abstract time-travel threat into mechanical urgency. Boss progression through history (1250→1650→1860→1943→3000) creates visual journey through human timeline while fighting backwards through invasion timeline. Weapon acquisition by era reflects technological progression narrative alongside mechanical scaling.
